title: Hieracium runcinatolobatum
taxonomy: Hieracium runcinatolobatum is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which is commonly known as hawkweed. The species runcinatolobatum is likely a member of the subgenus Pilosella. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is an angiosperm, or flowering plant. Hieracium runcinatolobatum's classification may also include further details within the family, subfamily, and tribe levels.
benefits: Unfortunately, I don't have information about the health benefits of Hieracium runcinatolobatum.
intro: Hieracium runcinatolobatum, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and lobed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium runcinatolobatum include sesquiterpene lactones, such as runcinatolobatin, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium runcinatolobatum is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive problems and skin infections.
